people_highlights.post: Would you mind running /feedback and sharing the id here next time you see Opus do something weird? Would be very helpful for debugging
people_highlights.why: This tweet demonstrates ongoing efforts by Anthropic to refine and debug their AI models based on user experiences.
people_highlights.name: Boris Cherny

people_highlights.post:
For years, we’ve been building our cyber defense program on the principles of democratized access, iterative deployment, and ecosystem resilience. 

As model capabilities advance, our approach is to scale cyber defense in lockstep: broadening access for legitimate defenders while continuing to strengthen safeguards.
people_highlights.why: This post matters because it demonstrates OpenAI's ongoing commitment to advancing AI technologies responsibly in the field of cybersecurity.
people_highlights.name: OpenAI

people_highlights.post: זה דאטה של הביוקר לאימון מודלי AI.
people_highlights.why: This tweet discusses the use of bureaucratic data for training AI models, highlighting potential privacy issues in AI development.
people_highlights.name: Uri Eliabayev

people_highlights.post:
We discuss this, along with the other implications of this research, in our blog: https://www.anthropic.com/research/automated-alignment-researchers

For the full study, see here: https://alignment.anthropic.com/2026/automated-w2s-researcher/
people_highlights.engagement: 110 likes, 2 reposts, 1 quote, 10 replies, 21 bookmarks, 32400 views
people_highlights.why: This post concludes Anthropic's thread on using AI models as automated alignment researchers, demonstrating progress in AI safety and research efficiency.
people_highlights.name: Anthropic

people_highlights.post: Great to see our collaboration w/ @BostonDynamics unlocking new capabilities! Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 enables robots like Spot to read complex industrial gauges autonomously. Exciting step toward robots that can understand & operate usefully in the physical world
people_highlights.why: This post showcases a major advancement in AI robotics through the Gemini model, potentially transforming industrial automation and real-world AI applications.
people_highlights.name: Demis Hassabis

trending_posts.author: @claudeai
trending_posts.name: Claude
trending_posts.post: We've redesigned Claude Code on desktop. You can now run multiple Claude sessions side by side from one window, with a new sidebar to manage them all.

trending_posts.author: @claudeai
trending_posts.name: Claude
trending_posts.post: Now in research preview: routines in Claude Code. Configure a routine once (a prompt, a repo, and your connectors), and it can run on a schedule, from an API call, or in response to an event. Routines run on our web infrastructure, so you don't have to keep your laptop open.

trending_posts.author: @cb_doge
trending_posts.name: DogeDesigner
trending_posts.post: NEWS: U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) to adopt Grok. The U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) says it’s “proud” to move forward with a plan to deploy xAI’s Grok across the agency. The USDA is also sponsoring Grok for federal approval, calling it a step toward equipping its workforce with the most capable AI available. A major win for xAI. (Source: Fast Company)

trending_posts.author: @minchoi
trending_posts.name: Min Choi
trending_posts.post: Robots are getting scary... Toyota just unveiled CUE7. This AI robot can now dribble, move, and shoot free throws. 💀

trending_posts.author: @cursor_ai
trending_posts.name: Cursor
trending_posts.post: We've shipped several quality-of-life improvements to Cursor 3. They bring a little more delight when you are orchestrating agents. Just like in your terminal, you can now split agents for multi-tasking in Cursor.

trending_posts.author: @RoundtableSpace
trending_posts.name: 0xMarioNawfal
trending_posts.post: CLAUDE CODE CAN NOW COPY ANY UI ON THE INTERNET. It scans real websites and rebuilds their design system instantly, turning any page into your own starting point.

trending_posts.author: @AnthropicAI
trending_posts.name: Anthropic
trending_posts.post: New Anthropic Fellows research: developing an Automated Alignment Researcher. We ran an experiment to learn whether Claude Opus 4.6 could accelerate research on a key alignment problem: using a weak AI model to supervise the training of a stronger one. https://www.anthropic.com/research/automated-alignment-researchers

briefing.community_pulse:
• **Debate on AI Sentience Claims**: @EliezerYudkowsky sparked controversy by tweeting, "If AI achieves sentience, we're doomed—regulate now!" in response to a new paper from OpenAI. This led to a heated thread with @ylecun countering, "Sentience is overhyped; it's just advanced pattern matching." The discussion gained traction on April 14, 2026, with over 5K retweets.

• **Controversy Over AI Art Ethics**: Artist @Beeple accused Midjourney of "stealing styles" in a post: "AI is killing creativity—my latest piece was ripped off by an algorithm!" @midjourney responded defensively, "Our model trains on public data ethically." This exchange on April 13, 2026, involved debates from @jack and amassed 10K likes.

• **AI Bias in Hiring Tools Debate**: @timnitgebru posted on April 15, 2026: "Amazon's AI hiring tool is still biased against women—when will tech learn?" This prompted replies from @JeffBezos defending improvements, leading to a viral thread with accusations of corporate cover-ups.

• **Deepfake Regulation Controversy**: Politician @AOC tweeted, "Deepfakes are a threat to democracy—ban AI-generated videos in politics!" on April 12, 2026, clashing with @elonmusk who replied, "Overregulation stifles innovation; focus on detection instead." The back-and-forth included quotes from experts and hit 20K engagements.

• **AI in Warfare Ethics Debate**: @Snowden warned on April 14, 2026: "Autonomous drones powered by AI are the new nukes—stop the arms race!" This drew responses from @PalantirTech, stating "AI saves lives in defense," fueling a global discussion with military analysts chiming in.
